capabilities.description: Shared editor chrome, icons, resources, style tokens, editor-only UI Toolkit helpers.
Editor-only build workflow registration, Build Profile policies and drift validation, build execution, artifact manifests, and build-manager presentation.
Shared game content authoring editor window, provider registration/discovery, validation display, asset path helpers, and creation-result UI.
General HTTP/API request construction, transport, serialization, authentication, and cancellation.
Authenticated session lifecycle, restore, refresh, logout, and persistence contracts.
Object, scene, and AssetBundle loading lifecycle and handles.
Generic keyed repositories, selection state, and repository primitives.
Collection-to-UI synchronization and presentation binding.
Reusable UI Toolkit and uGUI glass panels, icon buttons, control islands, scrubbers, and presentation chrome.
Reusable UI motion profiles, animated visibility, and cancellable icon/content reveal helpers.
UI routing, screens, channels, modals, guards, transitions, and back navigation.
Keyed world-object selection, hover tracking, raycast adapters, and highlight hooks.
Runtime palettes, theme assets, color roles, and UI theme adapters.
XR-ready world-space uGUI pressable controls, poke affordances, and world canvas event-camera helpers.
Camera pose capture/apply, bounds framing, camera movement, orbit/fly controls, projection transitions, and waypoint navigation.
Diagnostics providers, snapshots, JSON export, overlays, and aggregation.
Package discovery, install/update/remove, channels, registry composition, and ecosystem visualization.
Catalog, category hierarchy, dependency metadata, Integration metadata, Suite metadata, and governance metadata.
Safe destruction of transient UnityEngine.Object instances across Play Mode and Edit Mode.

exceptions.reason: Owns the exact Play Mode/Edit Mode implementation.
Explicit fixture teardown remains local; no testing package is approved.

capabilities.description: Shared normalized easing presets used by Deucarian package-owned motion systems.
